analysis.summary: Metabase is an open-source business intelligence and data analytics web application that enables non-technical users to create dashboards, ask questions of their data, and generate insights without SQL knowledge. Built with React/TypeScript frontend and Clojure backend, it uses a monolithic architecture with plugin-based extensions for enterprise features. The system supports multiple database connections, provides visual query building, SQL editing, and offers embedding capabilities through an SDK for integration into other applications.
capabilities: Visual query builder for non-technical users to create questions without SQL (frontend/src/metabase/query_builder)
SQL editor with syntax highlighting and auto-completion for advanced queries (frontend/src/metabase/sql_query_editor)
Interactive dashboard creation with filtering, auto-refresh, and custom click behaviors (frontend/src/metabase/dashboard)
Embedding SDK for integrating Metabase components into external applications (enterprise/frontend/src/embedding-sdk-package)
Multi-database connectivity with official and community drivers (modules/drivers/)
AI-powered query assistance through Metabot for natural language to SQL generation (frontend/src/metabase/metabot)
Real-time dashboard subscriptions via email and Slack (frontend/src/metabase/pulse)
Alert system for automated data notifications based on conditions (frontend/src/metabase/alert)
Data transformation pipeline for ETL operations (frontend/src/metabase/data_studio)
Permission system with granular access control and row-level security (enterprise/frontend/src/metabase-enterprise/advanced_permissions)
API key management for programmatic access (frontend/src/metabase/admin/settings)
Custom maps and geospatial visualization support (frontend/src/metabase/visualizations)
Multi-language internationalization with translation support (locales/)
Document creation for long-form data analysis with commenting (frontend/src/metabase/documents)
Data modeling tools for creating metrics and segments (frontend/src/metabase/admin/datamodel)
Collection management for organizing and sharing content (frontend/src/metabase/collections)
User management with SSO integration including SAML support (enterprise/frontend/src/metabase-enterprise/auth)
Caching layer for query performance optimization (resources/frontend_shared/src/metabase/lib/cache)
Migration system for database schema changes (resources/migrations/)
Plugin architecture for extending functionality (enterprise/frontend/src/metabase-enterprise/*/plugins)
Cross-database joins for querying across multiple data sources (frontend/src/metabase/lib/queries)
Export functionality for CSV, Excel, JSON, and PDF formats (frontend/src/metabase/sharing)
Public sharing and embedding with signed URLs (frontend/src/metabase/public)
Audit logging for tracking user activities and system changes (enterprise/backend/src/metabase_enterprise/audit_app)
Data sandboxing for multi-tenant deployments (enterprise/backend/src/metabase_enterprise/sandbox)
use_cases: Business analysts use Metabase to create interactive dashboards and reports from company databases without writing SQL
Data teams embed Metabase components into customer-facing applications to provide self-service analytics
Organizations deploy Metabase for company-wide business intelligence with role-based access to sensitive data
SaaS companies integrate Metabase's embedding SDK to offer white-labeled analytics to their customers
Executives and managers use automated dashboard subscriptions to receive regular business updates via email or Slack
analysis.target_audience: Business analysts, data teams, product managers, executives, and developers building analytics-enabled applications
technical_stack: Angular 20.0.0 for SDK host app examples (e2e/embedding-sdk-host-apps/angular-20-host-app/package.json)
api_endpoints: OpenAPI specification available at resources/openapi/openapi.json with comprehensive REST API documentation
Dashboard API endpoints for CRUD operations (resources/openapi/openapi.json)
Database connection API for data source management (resources/openapi/openapi.json)
Google Sheets integration API with sync capabilities (resources/openapi/openapi.json gsheets.response schema)
auth_methods: SAML SSO integration for enterprise authentication (enterprise/frontend/src/embedding/auth-common/saml)
API key authentication for programmatic access (e2e/support/helpers/api/createApiKey.ts)
integrations: Google Sheets for importing spreadsheet data (docs/cloud/google-sheets.md, resources/openapi/openapi.json)
Slack for dashboard subscriptions and notifications (docs/configuring-metabase/slack.md)
LDAP for user authentication and management (test_resources/ldap/)
Multiple database drivers including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB (modules/drivers/)
Git integration for version control sync (docs/cloud/migrate/)

architecture.data_flow: User queries flow through the frontend query builder to the Clojure backend, which processes MBQL (Metabase Query Language) and translates to native database queries. Results are cached and returned as JSON, with the frontend handling visualization and dashboard rendering. Enterprise features use a plugin architecture that extends core functionality.
architecture.persistence: Supports H2, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and other databases for application metadata storage. User data remains in connected source databases with query results optionally cached in application database.
architecture.concurrency_model: Backend uses Clojure's async/concurrent processing with core.async, frontend uses React's concurrent features and async/await patterns
architecture.communication_patterns: HTTP REST API between frontend and backend, WebSocket connections for real-time features, plugin system uses dependency injection patterns
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Enterprise features implemented as plugins that extend core functionality through defined hooks and interfaces, with separate frontend and backend plugin directories

primary_entities: Metrics and segments for data modeling
storage_backends: H2 database for development and small deployments
Connected databases remain as source of truth for user data
data_model.schema_patterns: Relational schema for application metadata with foreign key relationships between entities like dashboards, cards, and collections. User data follows source database schemas.
data_model.migration_strategy: Liquibase-based migration system with versioned YAML files in resources/migrations/ directory, supports both up and down migrations

algorithms.problem_type: Business intelligence query processing, data visualization, and multi-tenant analytics with permission-based data access
core_patterns: Query builder pattern for MBQL generation
Strategy pattern for different database drivers
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Complex query optimization engine, multi-database join processing, permission system with row-level security, plugin dependency management
key_algorithms: MBQL (Metabase Query Language) parsing and translation (src/metabase/query_processor/)
Permission resolution algorithm for data access control (enterprise/backend/src/metabase_enterprise/advanced_permissions/)
algorithms.evidence: src/metabase/query_processor/middleware/ contains query processing pipeline, frontend/src/metabase/query_builder/ implements visual query construction

security_model.authentication: Multiple methods including JWT tokens, SAML SSO, LDAP, and session-based auth with configurable providers
security_model.authorization: Role-based access control (RBAC) with granular permissions at database, schema, table, and row levels through enterprise sandboxing features
security_model.data_protection: TLS/SSL for data in transit, API key rotation, signed URLs for public sharing, configurable session timeouts
security_model.security_headers: CORS configuration support, CSP headers for embedding, rate limiting capabilities
security_model.vulnerability_handling: SECURITY.md file present, dependency scanning through automated tools, security audit logging in enterprise edition

infrastructure: PostgreSQL or MySQL for application database

deployment.container_orchestration: Docker Compose supported with provided docker-compose.yml files
deployment.ci_cd: GitHub Actions workflows for automated testing and deployment
deployment.environment_config: Environment variables for configuration (.env.example), Java system properties, and config file support
deployment.scaling_model: Horizontal scaling through multiple container instances with load balancer, database connection pooling for performance

code_quality_signals.test_framework: Jest for frontend unit tests, Cypress for E2E testing, Clojure test framework for backend
code_quality_signals.code_style: ESLint, Prettier for JavaScript/TypeScript, cljfmt for Clojure formatting

project_health.license: AGPL-3.0 for open source edition, Metabase Commercial License for enterprise features
project_health.top_contributors: Nicolò Pretto and core Metabase team
project_health.recent_activity: Active development with recent focus on fixing embedding SDK flaky tests and improving test reliability
project_health.community_signals: Security policy present, contributing guidelines available, code of conduct implied, issue templates likely present

information_gaps: Specific cloud provider integrations beyond AWS

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: The system implements a plugin architecture with enterprise features as modular extensions, supports multiple database drivers as pluggable modules, and provides an embedding SDK for external applications. The architecture explicitly separates core functionality from extensible components through defined hooks and interfaces.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Evidence: Comprehensive OpenAPI specification at resources/openapi/openapi.json, TypeScript type definitions throughout frontend, Clojure schemas for backend, and structured MBQL (Metabase Query Language) for query representation
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Evidence from MBQL parsing and translation pipeline in src/metabase/query_processor/, visual query builder generates tree structures, cross-database join optimization requires AST manipulation, and filter propagation follows tree traversal patterns
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Evidence from Clojure core.async implementation, query result caching with invalidation strategies, real-time dashboard updates via WebSocket, and multi-tenant deployments with sandboxing
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Transforms user-defined structured queries into optimized database-specific operations while maintaining a cached materialized view layer and supporting multi-tenant access control with real-time synchronization.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: Query AST construction → middleware pipeline processing → database-specific translation → execution → result caching → visualization rendering with bidirectional filter propagation
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The query processing pipeline, plugin architecture, and permission system are highly generalizable patterns. The MBQL-to-SQL translation engine could be adapted for any query transformation needs. However, the visualization components and dashboard UI are somewhat domain-specific. The caching layer, multi-tenant sandboxing, and embedding SDK represent reusable infrastructure components that could serve many analytical applications.

evidence_claims.claim: Implements sophisticated query AST processing pipeline
evidence_claims.evidence: src/metabase/query_processor/middleware/ contains query processing pipeline components

evidence_claims.claim: Comprehensive REST API with OpenAPI specification
evidence_claims.evidence: resources/openapi/openapi.json with comprehensive REST API documentation

cross_industry_applications: DevOps monitoring platforms requiring query builders for log/metric analysis
Financial services creating custom reporting tools for regulatory compliance
Healthcare systems building patient data analytics with HIPAA-compliant access controls
E-commerce platforms providing merchant analytics dashboards with multi-tenant isolation
Educational institutions creating student performance analytics with role-based permissions
IoT platforms offering device data visualization with real-time alerting
Content management systems providing usage analytics with embedded reporting
Supply chain management platforms creating operational dashboards with cross-database joins

complexity_factors: Complex query processing middleware pipeline requires deep understanding
Multi-database driver architecture needs careful abstraction layer design
Permission system with row-level security involves intricate access control logic
Enterprise plugin system requires understanding of dependency injection patterns
friction_report.migration_path: Extract the query processor engine and plugin host architecture, implement domain-specific query language, adapt the caching and permission layers, and build new visualization components while reusing the embedding SDK pattern

comparable_systems: Grafana - Plugin-based visualization platform with query processing for time-series databases
Jupyter Hub - Multi-tenant notebook environment with plugin architecture and embedded rendering
Apache Superset - Open-source data visualization platform with SQL translation and multi-database support
Retool - Low-code application builder with query abstraction layer and embedding capabilities
Observable - Collaborative data analysis platform with reactive query processing and sharing features
information_gaps: Specific query optimization algorithms and performance benchmarks
Detailed enterprise licensing model and feature boundaries
Exact caching invalidation strategies and cache hit ratios
Specific multi-tenant isolation mechanisms beyond row-level security
